title: Semantic Search: Vector Store Implementation
impact: Efficient storage and retrieval of embeddings; Enables similarity search at scale

cosineSimilarity.target: O(n) brute force, optimized inner loop

tasks.description: Define Vector Store Interface - Create IVectorStore interface with add(), search(), remove(), and size() methods; Define VectorSearchResult interface
acceptanceCriteria: size() method returning number of vectors
VectorSearchResult with name and score fields

tasks.description: Implement In-Memory Vector Store - Create InMemoryVectorStore class with Map-based storage, implement brute-force cosine similarity search with optimized inner loop
acceptanceCriteria: cosineSimilarity() helper with optimized loop (no array methods)
search() returns top-k results sorted by similarity

tasks.description: Add SQLite Embedding Storage - Extend SQLiteStorage to store embeddings as BLOB, add embedding and embedding_model columns to entities table
acceptanceCriteria: ALTER TABLE adds embedding BLOB column
ALTER TABLE adds embedding_model TEXT column
ALTER TABLE adds embedding_updated_at TEXT column

tasks.description: Implement SQLite Vector Store - Create SQLiteVectorStore that persists vectors to SQLite and loads into memory for search
acceptanceCriteria: Loads vectors into InMemoryVectorStore on init
Lazy loading option for large graphs

tasks.description: Create Vector Store Factory - Implement factory that creates InMemoryVectorStore or SQLiteVectorStore based on storage type
testCategories: SQLite storage uses SQLite vector store

tasks.description: Create Vector Store Tests - Test both implementations with various vector sizes and search scenarios
acceptanceCriteria: Test cosine similarity with known values

successCriteria: Factory creates appropriate store based on config
notes: The vector store provides efficient storage and retrieval of embedding vectors. For small graphs (<10K entities), brute-force cosine similarity is fast enough. For larger graphs, consider sqlite-vss extension in the future. SQLite storage persists embeddings so they don't need to be recomputed on each startup.
